name: What to Do Immediately After a Utah Construction Site Accident

step.name: Step 1
step.text: A construction site is an inherently dangerous environment. When an accident happens, the adrenaline and shock can make it difficult to think clearly. However, the actions you take in the first 24 hours can determine whether you receive the benefits you deserve or end up paying for medical bills out of pocket. Follow these steps to protect your health and your legal rights.

step.name: Step 3
step.text: Do not wait until the end of the day or until you see if the pain 'goes away.' In Utah and Idaho, the legal clock begins ticking as soon as the injury occurs. Tell your foreman, supervisor, or the site safety officer exactly what happened. Ensure they file a formal incident report and ask for a copy or take a photo of it. If you delay reporting, the insurance company will argue that the injury didn't actually happen on the clock.

step.name: Step 4
step.text: ### 2. Seek Medical Attention from an Authorized Provider

step.name: Step 5
step.text: In most cases, your employer or their insurance carrier has the right to choose the initial doctor you see. Ask your supervisor which clinic they use. When you arrive, tell the doctor your injury happened 'on the job.' This triggers the workers' compensation billing process. Be specific about all your symptoms—don't just focus on the most painful one. Minor neck pain at the scene could be a serious spinal issue that needs to be documented from the start.

step.name: Step 7
step.text: If you are physically able, or if you can ask a trusted coworker, take photos and videos of the accident scene. Capture the equipment involved, missing safety gear, weather conditions, or any cluttered walkways. Unlike an office injury, construction site conditions change hourly as work progresses. Your evidence could be gone by the next morning.

step.name: Step 9
step.text: Get the names and phone numbers of anyone who saw the accident or the conditions leading up to it. This includes subcontractors from other companies. Their testimony can be vital if your employer later claims the accident didn't happen the way you described.

step.name: Step 11
step.text: Insurance companies often assign adjusters to construction cases within hours. They may ask for a recorded statement to find ways to deny your claim or shift blame. Before giving a formal statement or signing any documents, consult with a lawyer who understands the specific nuances of Utah and Idaho workers' compensation laws. An attorney can ensure your average weekly wage is calculated correctly and that all potential sources of compensation—including third-party claims—are explored.

description: What to Do Immediately After a Utah Construction Site Accident - Murray
